comments:
Widget Types — Core types for dashboard widgets.

## Design Philosophy

Widgets are data visualization components for dashboards. Each widget
type has a corresponding data payload type that contains ALL information
needed to render the widget. No external state, no async loading within
the widget itself — the data is provided, the widget renders it.

This module defines:
- WidgetId — Deterministic identifier for widget instances
- WidgetType — Enumeration of all widget kinds
- RefreshRate — How often widget data should be refreshed
- ChangeDirection — Trend indicators (up/down/flat)
- Common formatting types

Individual widget data types (MetricData, ChartData, etc.) are defined
in their respective modules to keep this file focused and under 500 lines.

## Usage

```purescript
import Hydrogen.Element.Component.Widget.Types as Widget

-- Create a widget ID
myWidgetId = Widget.widgetId "dashboard-main" "revenue-metric"

-- Check widget type
case Widget.Metric of
  Widget.Metric -> "Displays a single KPI"
  Widget.Chart -> "Displays chart visualization"
  _ -> "Other widget type"
```

declarations.comments:
Unique identifier for a widget instance.

Generated deterministically from dashboard name + widget name.
Two widgets with the same dashboard and widget name will always
have the same WidgetId.
declarations.title: WidgetId

declarations.comments:
Create a widget ID from dashboard name and widget name.

Uses a namespaced approach to generate deterministic IDs:
`widget:dashboardName/widgetName` is hashed to produce a UUID.

```purescript
revenueWidget = widgetId "main-dashboard" "monthly-revenue"
usersWidget = widgetId "main-dashboard" "active-users"
```
declarations.title: widgetId

declarations.comments:
Create WidgetId from raw string (for deserialization).

Use with caution — this bypasses deterministic generation.
declarations.title: widgetIdFromString

declarations.comments: Unwrap widget ID to String.
declarations.title: unwrapWidgetId

declarations.comments: Convert to string for serialization.
declarations.title: widgetIdToString

declarations.comments:
Unique identifier for a dashboard.

Dashboards contain multiple widgets and have their own identity.
declarations.title: DashboardId

declarations.comments:
Create a dashboard ID from organization and dashboard name.

Uses a namespaced approach to generate deterministic IDs:
`dashboard:orgName/dashName` is hashed to produce a UUID.
declarations.title: dashboardId

declarations.comments: Create DashboardId from raw string.
declarations.title: dashboardIdFromString

declarations.comments: Unwrap dashboard ID.
declarations.title: unwrapDashboardId

declarations.comments: Convert to string.
declarations.title: dashboardIdToString

declarations.comments:
Types of dashboard widgets.

Each type corresponds to a specific visualization pattern:
- Metric: Single KPI with optional trend
- Chart: Line, bar, pie, etc. visualizations
- Table: Tabular data with sorting/pagination
- Gauge: Circular progress toward a target
- Sparkline: Inline mini chart
- Heatmap: Color-coded matrix
- Funnel: Conversion funnel
- Sankey: Flow diagram
- Treemap: Hierarchical boxes
- Map: Geographic visualization
declarations.title: WidgetType

declarations.comments: Convert widget type to string.
declarations.title: widgetTypeToString

declarations.comments: Parse widget type from string.
declarations.title: widgetTypeFromString

declarations.comments: All widget types for iteration.
declarations.title: allWidgetTypes

declarations.comments:
Direction of metric change.

Used to indicate whether a metric has increased, decreased, or
stayed flat compared to a previous period.
declarations.title: ChangeDirection

declarations.comments: Convert change direction to string.
declarations.title: changeDirectionToString

declarations.comments: Check if change is positive (up).
declarations.title: isPositiveChange

declarations.comments: Check if change is negative (down).
declarations.title: isNegativeChange

declarations.comments:
Widget refresh rate.

Controls how often the widget's data should be refreshed:
- RefreshRealtime: Push updates on every data change
- RefreshInterval: Poll at specified millisecond interval (bounded 1ms-24h)
- RefreshManual: Only refresh on explicit user request
declarations.title: RefreshRate

declarations.comments:
Refresh interval in milliseconds (1 to 86400000, i.e., 1ms to 24 hours).

Clamped to valid range on construction. Minimum 1ms prevents spinning,
maximum 24 hours is a reasonable upper bound for polling intervals.
declarations.title: RefreshMs

declarations.comments: Create a refresh interval, clamping to valid range (1-86400000).
declarations.title: mkRefreshMs

declarations.comments: Unwrap refresh milliseconds.
declarations.title: unwrapRefreshMs

declarations.comments:
Convert refresh rate to milliseconds.

Returns Nothing for realtime and manual (no polling interval).
declarations.title: refreshRateToMs

declarations.comments: Default refresh rate: 30 seconds.
declarations.title: defaultRefreshRate

declarations.comments:
Value formatting specification.

Tells the widget how to format numeric values for display.
declarations.title: ValueFormat

declarations.comments: Convert value format to string representation.
declarations.title: formatValueToString

declarations.comments:
Grid position for widget placement.

Widgets are placed on a grid (typically 12 columns).
Position specifies the (x, y) coordinate in grid cells.
declarations.title: GridPosition

declarations.comments: Create a grid position.
declarations.title: gridPosition

declarations.comments: Default position: top-left corner.
declarations.title: defaultGridPosition

declarations.comments:
Widget size in grid cells.

Width is in columns (typically 1-12), height is in rows.
declarations.title: WidgetSize

declarations.comments: Create a widget size.
declarations.title: widgetSize

declarations.comments: Small widget: 3 columns, 2 rows.
declarations.title: smallWidget

declarations.comments: Medium widget: 4 columns, 2 rows.
declarations.title: mediumWidget

declarations.comments: Large widget: 6 columns, 3 rows.
declarations.title: largeWidget

declarations.comments: Full width widget: 12 columns, 4 rows.
declarations.title: fullWidthWidget

declarations.comments:
Grid column (0-11 for 12-column grid).

Clamped to valid range on construction.
declarations.title: GridColumn

declarations.comments: Create a grid column, clamping to valid range (0-11).
declarations.title: mkGridColumn

declarations.comments: Unwrap grid column.
declarations.title: unwrapGridColumn

declarations.comments:
Grid row (0-99).

Clamped to valid range on construction.
declarations.title: GridRow

declarations.comments: Create a grid row, clamping to valid range (0-99).
declarations.title: mkGridRow

declarations.comments: Unwrap grid row.
declarations.title: unwrapGridRow

declarations.comments:
Percentage (0.0-100.0).

Clamped to valid range on construction.
declarations.title: Percentage

declarations.comments: Create a percentage, clamping to valid range (0-100).
declarations.title: mkPercentage

declarations.comments: Unwrap percentage.
declarations.title: unwrapPercentage

declarations.comments:
Decimal places (0-10).

Clamped to valid range on construction.
declarations.title: DecimalPlaces

declarations.comments: Create decimal places, clamping to valid range (0-10).
declarations.title: mkDecimalPlaces

declarations.comments: Unwrap decimal places.
declarations.title: unwrapDecimalPlaces

declarations.comments:
Check if a percentage is within a threshold range (inclusive).

Useful for gauge thresholds and progress indicators.
```purescript
isInRange (mkPercentage 50.0) (mkPercentage 25.0) (mkPercentage 75.0)  -- true
isInRange (mkPercentage 90.0) (mkPercentage 25.0) (mkPercentage 75.0)  -- false
```
declarations.title: isInRange

declarations.comments:
Check if a percentage exceeds a threshold.

Used for critical/warning threshold detection in gauges.
declarations.title: exceedsThreshold

declarations.comments:
Check if two widget types are the same.

Useful for filtering or grouping widgets by type.
declarations.title: isSameWidgetType

declarations.comments: Check if a widget type is a data visualization (Chart, Gauge, Sparkline, Heatmap).
declarations.title: isVisualization

declarations.comments: Check if a widget type displays flow/hierarchy (Funnel, Sankey, Treemap).
declarations.title: isFlowWidget

declarations.comments: Check if grid position is at origin.
declarations.title: isOrigin

declarations.comments:
Check if a widget fits within grid bounds.

Given a position and size, checks if the widget stays within
a 12-column grid.
declarations.title: fitsInGrid

name: Hydrogen.Element.Component.Widget.Types
